questions.title: Pod DNS policy and dnsConfig diagnostics
questions.question:
A pod DNS diagnostics brief is incomplete and contains stale or unsafe remediation guidance.

A ConfigMap named `dns-diagnostics-brief` already exists in namespace `dnspolicy-lab`. Repair it so that:
- `targetWorkload` is `dns-client`
- `podInventory` is `kubectl get pod dns-client -n dnspolicy-lab -o wide`
- `dnsPolicyCheck` is `kubectl get pod dns-client -n dnspolicy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.dnsPolicy}'`
- `dnsNameserverCheck` is `kubectl get pod dns-client -n dnspolicy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.dnsConfig.nameservers[0]}'`
- `dnsSearchCheck` is `kubectl get pod dns-client -n dnspolicy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.dnsConfig.searches[0]}'`
- `dnsOptionCheck` is `kubectl get pod dns-client -n dnspolicy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.dnsConfig.options[0].name}'`
- `resolverFileCheck` is `kubectl exec -n dnspolicy-lab dns-client -- cat /etc/resolv.conf`
- `eventCheck` is `kubectl get events -n dnspolicy-lab --sort-by=.lastTimestamp`
- `safeManifestNote` is `confirm dnsPolicy, dnsConfig nameservers, searches, and options before changing workload manifests or cluster DNS services`
- save the repaired ConfigMap manifest to `/tmp/exam/q1/dns-diagnostics-brief.yaml`
- save a plain-text checklist to `/tmp/exam/q1/dns-diagnostics-checklist.txt` with the sections `Pod DNS Inventory`, `dnsConfig Checks`, and `Safe Manifest Review`

Do not delete the Pod, do not restart the Deployment, and do not patch cluster DNS services during this drill.
